# Phantasm

Phantasm is a local, project-scoped memory runtime for AI agents. It is task-independent — any agent can use it for any kind of work, not only coding.
It bootstraps into each repository, stores durable runtime state under `.phantasm/`,
and exposes explicit operations for ingesting, searching, compiling, inspecting,
auditing, snapshotting, and maintaining project memory.

## Primary pages

- Home: /index.html
- Getting Started: /getting-started.html
- Configuration: /configuration.html
- Commands: /commands.html
- Why Phantasm: /why-phantasm.html
- Architecture: /architecture.html

## Key ideas

- Memory is project-scoped, not one global machine-wide memory bucket.
- Runtime state is local to each repository under `.phantasm/state/`.
- Durable truth lives in a single authoritative SQLite store per project.
- The top-level CLI exposes `bootstrap`/`init`, `agents --add`, `agents-plugin`, `dashboard`, human memory commands, and `handle-request`.
- Human memory commands include `list`, `show <id>`, `search "query"`, `add --kind <kind> --subject <key>`, `stats`, and `status`.
- `phm` is installed as a shorthand alias for the same CLI as `phantasm`.
- Requests use an explicit JSON envelope with versioned operations.
- handle-request input can be passed as one argv JSON string, `--stdin`, or `--file <path>`.
- Raw request input must be valid UTF-8; JSON strings support spec-compliant Unicode escapes.
- The `describe` operation returns machine-readable API schemas, safety rules, and runnable examples.
- Complete describe targets also return CLI alias and request transport metadata.
- Every response includes `notices`; release_update notices appear once per project/client profile after a binary update.
- Search uses deterministic token matching by default and supports exact or fuzzy discovery modes.
- Compile is deterministic by default and supports opt-in `explain` diagnostics for selected and omitted context candidates.
- Inspect with empty params returns a default memory summary with live records, scopes, lifecycle states, conflicts, review count, and timestamps.
- The read-only `templates` operation returns common record templates for project identity, architectural decisions, operational constraints, known issues, release procedures, environment setup, and deferred tasks.
- High-impact mutations require structured confirmations with approved_by, reason, operation, and a target matching the request.
- Ingest creates new memory; use revise to update existing records. Duplicate subject_key ingests are refused while a live record exists and return the existing record id with revise guidance.
- Trusted ingest callers can set `review_required=true` to create a suggestion and review item instead of authoritative memory.
- Batch mutations `ingest_many` and `tombstone_many` process multiple items in one request using per-item idempotency keys and per-item result status.
- Records and suggestions support first-class `quality` metadata for confidence, source, verification, expiry, revalidation, and authorship. Search and compile filters can target that metadata.
- `stale_review` is read-only freshness and changed-file impact review; `duplicate_review` is read-only semantic-overlap review with consolidation guidance.
- `memory_export` writes Git-reviewable canonical JSON or review-only Markdown to a project-relative path. `memory_import` additively loads reviewed canonical JSON; sensitive export requires explicit inclusion and an export-capable profile.
- `phantasm dashboard` launches a loopback-only, token-protected local dashboard with embedded offline UI, charts, graph exploration, and review actions.
- `phantasm agents-plugin` installs the bundled harness-agnostic `phantasm-memory` plugin into supported local harness plugin directories.

## Core CLI

```bash
phantasm --help
phm --help
phantasm help agents
phantasm --version
phm --version
phantasm bootstrap [project-path] [--agent-guidance] [--no-agent-plugin] [--agent-file <path>]
phantasm init [project-path] [--agent-guidance] [--no-agent-plugin] [--agent-file <path>]
phantasm agents --add [project-path] [--agent-file <path>]
phantasm agents-plugin [--target <target>] [--path <dir>] [--dry-run] [--force]
phantasm dashboard [--port <port>] [--no-open] [--profile <profile>]
phantasm list
phantasm show <id>
phantasm search "query"
phantasm add --kind decision --subject release-process
phantasm stats
phantasm status
phantasm dashboard [--port <PORT>] [--no-open] [--profile <PROFILE>]
phantasm handle-request '<json request envelope>'
phantasm handle-request --stdin
phantasm handle-request --file request.json
phantasm handle-request '{"operation":"describe","params":{"target":"all"}}'
phantasm handle-request '{"operation":"describe","params":{"target":"*"}}'
```

Rerun `phantasm agents --add` after Phantasm updates when managed agent
guidance changes. It refreshes the managed block in place, backs up
existing files, and preserves text outside the managed markers.
`phantasm bootstrap --agent-guidance` also installs the bundled
harness-agnostic `phantasm-memory` plugin by default; use
`--no-agent-plugin` to skip plugin installation.

## Common setup

```bash
brew install indefiniteloop/tap/phantasm
# or
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/indefiniteloop/phantasm-dist/main/scripts/install.sh?utm_source=phatasm | sh
phantasm --version
phm --version
cd /path/to/project
phantasm bootstrap --agent-guidance
phantasm agents-plugin --dry-run
```

## Runtime operations

- describe
- bootstrap
- templates
- ingest
- ingest_many
- revise
- tombstone
- tombstone_many
- archive
- promote
- resolve_conflict
- accept_suggestion
- reject_suggestion
- defer_review
- resolve_review
- search
- duplicate_review
- compile
- inspect
- audit
- review_queue
- stale_review
- health
- snapshot_export
- snapshot_import
- memory_export
- memory_import
- backup_list
- backup_restore
- maintenance_plan
- maintenance_run

Agents should call describe for machine-readable API discovery:

```bash
phantasm handle-request '{"operation":"describe","params":{"target":"all"}}'
phantasm handle-request '{"operation":"describe","params":{"target":"*"}}'
phantasm handle-request '{"operation":"describe","params":{"target":"ingest"}}'
printf '%s' '{"operation":"describe","params":{"target":"ingest"}}' | phantasm handle-request --stdin
```
